item.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket explodes during testing in Florida
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The explosion is likely a major setback for Jeff Bezos' spaceflight company, and its attempt to compete with SpaceX.

item.title: Glean’s top line crosses $300M as AI budget-cutting becomes its major selling point
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The enterprise AI search startup tripled its annual revenue even as tech giants entered the category.

item.title: Waymo dominates autonomous vehicle registrations as Tesla trails behind
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: A new law and AV tracker tool gives the clearest accounting yet of how many robotaxis and self-driving trucks are in Texas.

item.title: How to apply to Startup Battlefield 2026, what you need ahead of the June 8 deadline
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

item.title: The internet is being rebuilt for machines
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: As AI agents move from experiments to production, AWS, Cloudflare, and others are redesigning cloud infrastructure for a future dominated by machine-generated internet traffic instead of human users.

item.title: Slate Auto will announce pricing and take preorders for its EV on June 24
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The Bezos-backed EV startup has yet to announce final pricing for its vehicle, which is supposed to start shipping by the end of this year.

item.title: Asana acquires no-code agent-builder StackAI
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Asana will incorporate StackAI into its growing suite of AI workflow tools.

item.title: Bluesky embraces long-form content to counter X Articles
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: In its latest update, Bluesky is getting into long-form content.

item.title: Hackers are trying to steal Signal users’ backups in new wave of phishing attacks
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: A new hacking campaign is trying to trick Signal users to give up their secret recovery key, which can be used to access online backups containing past messages.

item.title: Anthropic raises $65 billion, nears $1T valuation ahead of IPO
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic has closed a $65 billion Series H round at a $965 billion post-money valuation, marking what could be the AI startup's final private fundraise before a highly anticipated IPO.

item.title: Just like gold and oil, we’ll soon be able to trade AI token futures
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Large exchanges are designing derivative products around AI tokens, which are increasingly being considered less a computational output and more a raw material input, like electricity or bandwidth.

item.title: H1 secures $40M from CVS, proving SaaS startups can still attract investment
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: CEO Ariel Katz argues that while AI can replicate workflow SaaS, it can't copy H1’s unique doctor data.

item.title: A security lapse at prison pay phone service Pay Tel publicly exposed over 300K callers’ driver’s licenses
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Pay Tel secured the publicly exposed data after security researchers discovered the leak containing callers' sensitive ID documents and inmate communications.

item.title: Why Paris may be the most important AI city outside Silicon Valley
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Europe’s startup ecosystem has matured significantly; its founders are increasingly willing to scale companies domestically instead of immediately looking to relocate to the U.S.

item.title: In just 3 weeks, StrictlyVC is coming to Los Angeles
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: StrictlyVC Los Angeles is on June 18. Join for meaningful networking and fireside chats with leaders from Mach Industries, Shinkei Systems, and more. Register today.

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline extended to June 8 after overwhelming demand
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: After overwhelming demand from founders around the world, TechCrunch has extended the Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline to June 8. Nominate a standout startup or apply yours today.

item.title: Corgi announces $106M raise at $2.6B valuation — double what it was worth 3 weeks ago
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: While startups raising back-to-back rounds at steep step-ups have become almost routine, a company whose valuation doubles in three weeks is unusual enough to raise questions, particularly given the investor set in both rounds is the same.

item.title: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new ‘dynamic workflow’ tool
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: The new Opus model comes with a tool called Dynamic Workflows, for coordinating swarms of subagents.

item.title: Waymo’s newest robotaxi is Chinese-made, built to make money, and now accepting riders
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The launch of the Ojai minivan robotaxi comes after years of development and testing, but arrives amid a challenging time for Waymo.

item.title: US says troops were targeted with location data, as senator warns ad industry is a ‘national security threat’
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: One leading privacy lawmaker said it was time to "start treating the adtech industry as a national security threat."
